title: Who's Who in the Ancient World
excerpt: A comprehensive biographical dictionary of the ancient world, covering Greek and Roman figures from mythology and history, with entries on statesmen, philosophers, writers, artists, and key mythological characters.
keyIdeas: The ancient world produced figures whose ideas and achievements continue to shape Western civilization
Biography is an essential lens for understanding classical history and culture
Mythological and historical figures are intertwined in ancient sources and must be understood together
keyTakeaways: Coverage spans from the Mycenaean period (c. 1600 BCE) to the fall of the Western Roman Empire (476 CE)
Entries include mythological figures alongside historical statesmen, writers, and philosophers
Each entry provides biographical information, historical context, and assessment of the figure's significance
Cross-references link related figures and connect mythological to historical subjects
The book includes genealogical tables of major mythological families
whoShouldRead: Students of classics and ancient history
General readers wanting to identify figures encountered in classical literature
Anyone who encounters references to ancient figures and wants quick biographical context
whoShouldSkip: Specialists who need more detailed scholarly treatment
Readers focused on non-Greco-Roman ancient civilizations
Those seeking in-depth analysis rather than concise reference entries
